perturbation.original_text: Party B warrants that none of the articles, contents and web pages of the Cooperation channel is in violation of PRC laws or any international treaty to which the PRC is a signatory, including without limitation any content detrimental to national security, of pornographic, fraudulent, insulting, defamatory, hectoring or harassing nature, infringing upon the copyrights, personal rights or any other valid rights and interests of any other parties or in breach of any social customs, or any linkage thereto. If Party A receives any complaint regarding the content of the Cooperation channel, Party B shall resolve such complaint immediately, negotiate with or respond to any review or enquiry from any third party or competent authorities at its own expenses, and be liable for any loss incurred by Party A.
perturbation.changed_text: Party B warrants that none of the articles, contents and web pages of the Cooperation channel is in violation of PRC laws or any international treaty to which the PRC is a signatory, including without limitation any content detrimental to national security, of pornographic, fraudulent, insulting, defamatory, hectoring or harassing nature, infringing upon the copyrights, personal rights or any other valid rights and interests of any other parties or in breach of any social customs, or any linkage thereto. If Party A receives any complaint regarding the content of the Cooperation channel, Party A may, at its sole discretion, forward the complaint to Party B. Party B shall resolve such complaint immediately, negotiate with or respond to any review or enquiry from any third party or competent authorities at its own expenses, and be liable for any loss incurred by Party A.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of the phrase "Party A may, at its sole discretion, forward the complaint to Party B" introduces uncertainty. The original text implies that Party A is obligated to inform Party B of complaints. The modified text gives Party A the option to not inform Party B, potentially delaying resolution or leading to a breach. This contradicts the later requirement for Party B to resolve the complaint immediately, creating ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Party B warrants that none of the articles, contents and web pages of the Cooperation channel is in violation of PRC laws or any international treaty to which the PRC is a signatory, including without limitation any content detrimental to national security, of pornographic, fraudulent, insulting, defamatory, hectoring or harassing nature, infringing upon the copyrights, personal rights or any other valid rights and interests of any other parties or in breach of any social customs, or any linkage thereto. If Party A receives any complaint regarding the content of the Cooperation channel, Party A may, at its sole discretion, forward the complaint to Party B. Party B shall resolve such complaint immediately, negotiate with or respond to any review or enquiry from any third party or competent authorities at its own expenses, and be liable for any loss incurred by Party A.
perturbation.justification: The added clause "Party A may, at its sole discretion, forward the complaint to Party B" creates a contradiction with the requirement that "Party B shall resolve such complaint immediately". Party A's discretion to forward or not forward the complaint introduces a potential delay in Party B being notified, thus creating a conflict with Party B's obligation to resolve the complaint immediately. This aligns with the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type, as the added clause introduces conflicting implications regarding the complaint resolution process.

perturbation.original_text: Party A agrees that the pricing, specifications and contents of the advertising on the Cooperation Channel is subject to sole discretion of Party B. Party B has absolute and sole control, approval and discretion regarding the advertising operation of the Cooperation Channel, may conduct and benefit from legal advertising operations at its sole discretion.
perturbation.changed_text: Party A agrees that the pricing, specifications and contents of the advertising on the Cooperation Channel is subject to sole discretion of Party B, unless otherwise dictated by Party A. Party B has absolute and sole control, approval and discretion regarding the advertising operation of the Cooperation Channel, may conduct and benefit from legal advertising operations at its sole discretion.
perturbation.explanation: By adding "unless otherwise dictated by Party A" the absolute discretion of Party B over advertising is removed. This undermines the statement that Party B has 'absolute and sole control' which is later reffered in the section and introduce uncertainty. This creates a contradiction about the extent of Party B's authority.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Party A agrees that the pricing, specifications and contents of the advertising on the Cooperation Channel is subject to sole discretion of Party B, unless otherwise dictated by Party A. Party B has absolute and sole control, approval and discretion regarding the advertising operation of the Cooperation Channel, may conduct and benefit from legal advertising operations at its sole discretion.
perturbation.justification: The addition of "unless otherwise dictated by Party A" directly undermines the initial assertion of Party B's "absolute and sole control, approval and discretion" over advertising operations. The changed text creates a clear conflict by allowing Party A to override Party B's discretion, which contradicts the original clause's intent of granting Party B ultimate authority. The contradiction exists within the same text as the original and changed text, introducing uncertainty regarding the extent of Party B's authority.

perturbation.original_text: Party A maintains its ownership of all rights, entitlements and interests of its websites and trademarks. Party B maintains its ownership of all rights, entitlements and interests of its websites, trademarks, and the information and data on the Cooperation Channel.
perturbation.changed_text: Party A maintains its ownership of all rights, entitlements and interests of its websites and trademarks. Party B maintains its ownership of all rights, entitlements and interests of its websites, trademarks, and the information and data on the Cooperation Channel, except for user data, which is jointly owned.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of "except for user data, which is jointly owned" creates a contradiction. The original text clearly states Party B's ownership of all information and data on the Cooperation Channel. The modified text now introduces joint ownership of user data, which is a subset of the data on the channel. This is in direct contradiction of Party B ownership of all the information, since user data belongs to the information set.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Party A maintains its ownership of all rights, entitlements and interests of its websites and trademarks. Party B maintains its ownership of all rights, entitlements and interests of its websites, trademarks, and the information and data on the Cooperation Channel, except for user data, which is jointly owned.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that Party B owns all information and data on the Cooperation Channel. The changed text modifies this to state that Party B owns all information and data on the Cooperation Channel, except for user data, which is jointly owned. This creates a direct contradiction because 'user data' is a subset of 'information and data on the Cooperation Channel'. The contradicted text is the changed text. The change directly breaks compatibility, causing a contradiction, because the changed text now states user data is jointly owned, when before it stated Party B maintains ownership of ALL data.
